mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.3pp unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative or flat 20-day equity returns during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 220 signal entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 220 consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contractions in real GDP and retail sales confirm a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Simultaneous declines in GDP and retail sales fail to predict negative 20-day returns (hit rate < 40%).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a supply-constrained environment signals capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative 5-day returns during supply-constrained periods.
